question.label: Improvement loop
question.name: What would a tighter one-skill feedback loop look like in practice?
question.description: Final cause-stage question to make the missing feedback structure concrete before closing on commitment.

answer: If we align on concrete criteria, I can work against them confidently.

assessment.note: He names concrete criteria alignment as the missing condition for confident execution, which is a positive clarity and growth signal; wellbeing was not engaged.

new_queue.label: Practice commitment
new_queue.name: What one habit do you want to practice before we meet again?

new_queue.label: Practice commitment
new_queue.name: Given what we've covered, where do you want to focus first?

issues: coverage: injected wellbeing into next question (0 touches after turn 8)

history.answer_excerpt: I can name wins, but I am not always sure they match the team bar.
Feedback has helped, though I still miss consistency under time pressure.
I can name wins, but I am not always sure they match the team bar.
Feedback has helped, though I still miss consistency under time pressure.
I sometimes focus on polish and lose sight of the core message.
I want clearer examples of what excellent junior-level work looks like here.
When reviews are broad, I am not sure what to change first.
I would like to grow faster in concept rationale, not just execution speed.
A tighter loop on one skill at a time would help me improve.
If we align on concrete criteria, I can work against them confidently.
